title: Mathikettan Shola National Park
tagline: Mathikettan Shola National Park — wildlife reserve of Kerala

overview.short: Mathikettan Shola National Park is a 12.82 km2 national park in Santhanpara village of Udumbanchola taluk in Idukki district of India's Kerala state.
overview.description: Mathikettan Shola National Park is a 12.82 km2 national park in Santhanpara village of Udumbanchola taluk in Idukki district of India's Kerala state. It was notified as a national park on 21 November 2003. Considering the unique nature of the shola forest in Mathikettan and its importance as an elephant strip, the state government has declared it as a National park in 2008. Central government has notified its area varying from zero to one kilometre around the boundary as eco sensitive zone.

topPlaces.name: Devimala
topPlaces.description: Devimala, is one of the high peaks of Western Ghats in the (Devikulam) taluk of Idukki district of Kerala. Devimala Peak lies in the Anamalai hills of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Chinnakanal
topPlaces.description: Chinnakanal is a village in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. It has waterfalls which are popularly known as the Power House Waterfalls.

topPlaces.name: Devikulam
topPlaces.description: Devikulam is a Grama Panchayat and small hill station about 8 kilometres (5.0 mi) away from Munnar in the Idukki District of Kerala, India. It lies about 1,800 metres (5,900 ft) above sea level.

topPlaces.name: Devikulam taluk
topPlaces.description: Devikulam taluk is one of the 5 taluks in Idukki district of the Indian state of Kerala. Devikulam taluk comprises 13 villages.

topPlaces.name: Pottankad
topPlaces.description: Pottankad is a village in the Idukki district of Kerala, India. It is about 3020 ft.

topPlaces.name: Suryanelli
topPlaces.description: Suryanelli is a resort settlement in the Chinnakanal village in Idukki district, Kerala, India. Situated 40 km southeast of Munnar.

topPlaces.name: Carmelagiri Elephant Park
topPlaces.description: Carmelagiri Elephant Park is a private park for elephant riding through the hilly paths of Munnar forest, a part of Kerala tourism, India.

topPlaces.name: Kunchithanny
topPlaces.description: Kunchithanny is a village in the Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. The village is on the banks of the river Muthirappuzha.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mathikettan Shola National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mathikettan Shola National Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mathikettan Shola National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (81 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (93 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mathikettan Shola National Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mathikettan Shola National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mathikettan Shola National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Mathikettan Shola National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mathikettan Shola National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Devimala, Chinnakanal, Devikulam, Devikulam taluk, Pottankad.
